mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes GLE350d / GLE400d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned GLE350d / GLE400d (OM656) engine typically costs £5,000–£12,000 supply only depending on variant. A tandem oil/vacuum pump replacement can cost £4,500 or more. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 at independent specialist rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes GLE350d / GLE400d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM656 is generally considered a reliable engine and a very good engine with proper maintenance. However, it has serious vulnerabilities: the tandem oil/vacuum pump failure is the defining weakness — a £4,500+ engine-out job. Coolant ingress into the vacuum system is a safety-critical recall item. The 400d adds turbo vacuum pipe collapse and excessive oil consumption. Keep it under warranty.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes GLE350d / GLE400d?

acceptedAnswer.text: Tandem oil/vacuum pump failure (engine-out job, £4,500+), coolant ingress into the vacuum system (recall — fire risk), turbo actuator vacuum pipe collapse (400d), excessive oil consumption (400d), EGR and AdBlue system issues, and DPF blockage. See the Era Map above for detailed advice.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes GLE350d / GLE400d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept GLE400d (values £40,000–£70,000+ depending on year and mileage), a £6,000–£8,000 reconditioned engine is 10–20% of the vehicle's value — worth it for a solid example. The Mercedes scrap threshold of 70% means evaluating the whole vehicle and its condition before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes GLE350d / GLE400d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code OM656 is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The GLE350d produces 268–272 bhp, while the GLE400d produces 330 bhp. The OM656 is a 2.9-litre inline-six diesel, introduced in 2017. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm for free.

mainEntity.name: Has the Mercedes GLE350d / GLE400d been recalled for the tandem pump issue?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — Mercedes-Benz issued a recall for more than 800,000 vehicles with OM654 and OM656 engines due to a coolant pump leak that could allow coolant to enter the vacuum circuit. This could cause increased temperature, loss of braking assistance, and potential fire risk. Check your vehicle's recall status immediately — this is a safety-critical item.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes GLE350d / GLE400d Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: Budget priority; finding a good donor OM656 is challenging

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the GLE350d / GLE400d OM656 era (2019–present). A tandem oil/vacuum pump replacement can cost £4,500 or more, with timing chain access requiring engine removal for certain repairs.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a GLE350d / GLE400d engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £85–£130/hr (independent) or £140–£200/hr (London/SE dealer). The OM656's inline‑six layout and complex vacuum/oil pump system add significant labour. [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

cta.label: → Compare GLE350d / GLE400d engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Tandem Oil/Vacuum Pump Failure (OM656 — All Generations)
problems.symptoms: Worsening brake performance, loss of braking assistance, engine warning light (MIL), loss of power, gearbox becoming erratic, no more than 3,000 rpm, low mpg. Oil contamination in the brake‑vacuum system and coolant leakage into the engine oil system, leading to sludging.
problems.typicalMileage: 40,000–60,000 miles — can occur as early as 40,000 miles; one owner reported failure at 45,000 km

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you notice worsening brake performance, stop driving immediately

problems.recommendation: The tandem oil/vacuum pump failure is the single biggest reliability concern on the OM656. The pump is "buried in the middle of the engine and it's engine out/head off". Mercedes issued a recall for coolant pump leaks entering the vacuum system, which could result in a fire. One owner described it as "a very bad design by MB". A simple check is to locate the brake vacuum servo, remove the vacuum pipe and dip in a pipe cleaner — any signs of oil then walk away. If you own a GLE with the OM656, keep it under warranty — repairs are extraordinarily expensive.

problems.title: Turbo Actuator Vacuum Pipe Collapse (GLE400d)
problems.symptoms: Intermittent loss of power, gear shift judder on long journeys, limp mode with P0299E6 error code (boost pressure too low), no warning light until severe, transmission judder on gear changes.
problems.typicalMileage: 20,000–60,000 miles — one owner reported issues from 2.5 years old
problems.replacementCost: £5,500–£10,000 (if turbo failure leads to engine damage)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if power loss or judder occurs

problems.recommendation: A very common issue on the OM656 is the turbo actuator vacuum pipe collapsing due to excessive heat. The diagnostics are "dumb" — they only look for a vacuum being drawn and not the actuator responding correctly. This is a known issue with the OM656 and has nothing to do with the turbo itself but the vacuum pipes that actuate the turbo. If you experience intermittent power loss or gear shift judder, have the turbo vacuum system inspected. Some owners have had success replacing the vacuum lines and solenoids.

problems.title: Excessive Oil Consumption (GLE400d)
problems.symptoms: Low oil warning light, needing to top up oil frequently, oil consumption of 1L per 4,000 km or more.
problems.repairCost: £150–£300 (regular oil top‑ups and monitoring) — £1,000–£4,000+ (piston ring or engine inspection)【PLACEHOLDER】
problems.replacementCost: £5,500–£12,000 (if oil starvation leads to engine damage)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; monitor oil levels weekly

problems.recommendation: The GLE400d has been reported to consume significant amounts of oil. One owner reported adding 1 litre of oil every 4,000 km, with the dealer claiming 2 litres in 25,000 km is "normal". Mercedes considers oil consumption of 0.9 US quart per 600 miles (approximately 1L per 1,000 km) acceptable. Check your oil level regularly — this engine is not one you can neglect. If consumption exceeds 1L per 1,000 miles, seek professional diagnosis.

problems.title: Coolant Ingress into Vacuum System (OM656 Recall)
problems.symptoms: Engine warning light (MIL), loss of power, diminished brake booster function, potential fire risk.
problems.replacementCost: £5,000–£12,000 (if failure leads to engine damage or fire)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: check recall status immediately; can cause fire and brake failure

problems.recommendation: Mercedes‑Benz issued a recall for more than 800,000 vehicles with OM654 and OM656 engines due to a coolant pump leak that could allow coolant to enter the vacuum circuit. This could cause increased temperature, loss of braking assistance, and potential fire risk. If your GLE hasn't had this recall work completed, get it done immediately. This is a safety‑critical item. A software update and switchover valve replacement are part of the fix.

problems.title: EGR & AdBlue System Issues (OM656)
problems.symptoms: Engine warning light, reduced performance, increased emissions, AdBlue countdown timer, NOx sensor faults.
problems.repairCost: £300–£1,200 (EGR cleaning, AdBlue sensor or heater replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】
problems.replacementCost: £1,500–£3,000 (if DPF or SCR system requires replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if warning lights appear

problems.recommendation: The OM656's emissions systems are complex. The EGR system can become clogged, leading to reduced performance. AdBlue system issues with NOx sensors and tank heaters have been reported. The AdBlue system reliability has improved on later models as everything is now housed closer to the engine block, resulting in faster warm‑up. If you see warning lights, have the emissions system inspected promptly.

problems.title: DPF Blockage (OM656 — Short‑Journey Cars)
problems.symptoms: DPF warning light, reduced power, increased fuel consumption, frequent regeneration cycles.
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — urban‑driven cars are at highest risk
problems.repairCost: £200–£800 (DPF clean, forced regeneration, or sensor replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if DPF warning appears

problems.recommendation: Short trips or city driving can lead to DPF clogging, requiring regeneration or, in severe cases, replacement. If you see a DPF warning light, take the car for a sustained motorway run at 2,000–2,500 rpm. If the light persists, have the DPF inspected. Regular motorway driving and using premium diesel can help keep the DPF healthy.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Mercedes GLE350d / GLE400d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Essential repair if under warranty; out‑of‑warranty costs are eye‑watering
Catch it before turbo damage occurs
Viable if caught early; often requires engine inspection
Sensor and heater replacement is straightforward
Cleaning or forced regeneration is cost‑effective
OM656 failures are rare but catastrophic when they occur

buyingChecks.title: Buying This GLE350d / GLE400d? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with regular oil changes using Mercedes‑approved oil
* Tandem pump recall completed (check with Mercedes)
* Coolant pump recall completed (check with Mercedes)
* Turbo vacuum system inspected with no power loss issues
* Extended warranty in place — these cars are expensive to repair out of warranty
avoidIf: * No proof of regular maintenance — the OM656 is unforgiving of neglect
* No proof of recall work completed — tandem pump failure is a safety concern
* Any oil in the brake vacuum line — walk away
* Any power loss or turbo issues — expensive repairs ahead
* Any coolant loss warnings — recall work may be outstanding
* "Cheap" examples — the purchase price is the least of your worries

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Mercedes GLE350d and GLE400d are premium SUVs with a powerful and refined 2.9‑litre straight‑six diesel. The OM656 is "generally considered a reliable engine" and "a very good engine" with proper maintenance. However, it has serious vulnerabilities. The tandem oil/vacuum pump failure is the defining weakness — a £4,500+ engine‑out job. Coolant ingress into the vacuum system is a safety‑critical recall item. The 400d adds turbo vacuum pipe collapse and excessive oil consumption to the list. As one owner put it: "I certainly won't be buying into this Mercedes design failure". Buy on service history, prioritise a car with recall work completed, and absolutely maintain a warranty. This is a brilliant SUV — but it demands deep pockets and careful ownership.

cta.label: → Compare GLE350d / GLE400d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes GLE350d or GLE400d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Recall Status — Has the tandem pump and coolant pump recall been completed? (critical safety items)
* Brake Vacuum Test — Locate the brake vacuum servo, remove the vacuum pipe and dip in a pipe cleaner — any signs of oil? Walk away.
* Cold Start — Listen for any unusual noises (OM656 health indicator)
* Service History — Regular oil changes with Mercedes‑approved oil are absolutely essential
* Oil Level — Check dipstick; excessive consumption indicates potential piston ring wear
* Power Delivery — Test acceleration; any hesitation, turbo lag, or gear shift judder?
* Coolant Level — Check cold and hot; any loss indicates potential coolant pump issues
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Tandem pump, turbo, EGR, or AdBlue codes are most common

items.label: SECTION 8 — FAQ
items.value: 1. How much does a Mercedes GLE350d / GLE400d engine replacement cost in the UK?

items.label: A reconditioned GLE350d / GLE400d (OM656) engine typically costs £5,000–£12,000 supply only depending on variant. A tandem oil/vacuum pump replacement can cost £4,500 or more. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800 at independent specialist rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.
items.value: 2. Is the Mercedes GLE350d / GLE400d engine reliable?

items.label: The OM656 is "generally considered a reliable engine" and "a very good engine" with proper maintenance. However, it has serious vulnerabilities: the tandem oil/vacuum pump failure is the defining weakness — a £4,500+ engine‑out job. Coolant ingress into the vacuum system is a safety‑critical recall item. The 400d adds turbo vacuum pipe collapse and excessive oil consumption. As one owner put it: "I certainly won't be buying into this Mercedes design failure".
items.value: 3. What are the common problems with the Mercedes GLE350d / GLE400d?

items.label: Tandem oil/vacuum pump failure (engine‑out job, £4,500+), coolant ingress into the vacuum system (recall — fire risk), turbo actuator vacuum pipe collapse (400d), excessive oil consumption (400d), EGR and AdBlue system issues, and DPF blockage. See the Era Map above for detailed advice.
items.value: 4. Is it worth replacing a Mercedes GLE350d / GLE400d engine, or should I scrap the car?

items.label: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well‑kept GLE400d (values £40,000–£70,000+ depending on year and mileage), a £6,000–£8,000 reconditioned engine is 10–20% of the vehicle's value — worth it for a solid example. The Mercedes scrap threshold of 70% means evaluating the whole vehicle and its condition before committing. Given the GLE's status as a premium SUV, engine replacement is often worth it for a good example — but only with a warranty.
items.value: 5. How do I find my Mercedes GLE350d / GLE400d engine code?

items.label: The engine code OM656 is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The GLE350d produces 268–272 bhp, while the GLE400d produces 330 bhp. The OM656 is a 2.9‑litre inline‑six diesel, introduced in 2017 and used across the GLE, GLS, S‑Class and E‑Class ranges. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm for free.
items.value: 6. Has the Mercedes GLE350d / GLE400d been recalled for the tandem pump issue?

items.label: Yes — Mercedes‑Benz issued a recall for more than 800,000 vehicles with OM654 and OM656 engines due to a coolant pump leak that could allow coolant to enter the vacuum circuit. This could cause increased temperature, loss of braking assistance, and potential fire risk. Check your vehicle's recall status immediately — this is a safety‑critical item.
